title: Use identity precompile (address 0x4) as a cheap memcpy in assembly
description: The EVM identity precompile at address 0x4 returns its input unchanged. staticcall(gas(), 4, src, len, dst, len) copies len bytes from src to dst — a free memcpy. Cheaper than a manual copy loop for medium-sized buffers (>32 bytes). The call overhead is ~700 gas base but each 32-byte word copied in a loop costs ~9 gas, so breakeven is around 80 bytes.
pattern_after:
// Identity precompile memcpy
if n { pop(staticcall(gas(), 4, add(data, 0x20), n, dst, n)) }
when_to_apply: Copying dynamic bytes in assembly when length > ~80 bytes
when_not_to_apply: Small fixed-size copies (≤64 bytes) where 1-2 mstore calls are cheaper than precompile call overhead
